paper_title: Ice Core Chronology and Error Correlation Analysis

methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of Datice experiments to calculate coherent chronologies for ice and gas phases using palaeo-observations and background parameters.

steps.description: Provide palaeo-observations and background parameters T b k (z), A b k (z), and C b k (z) with their respective uncertainties.
steps.searchable_summary: input palaeo-observations and background parameters with uncertainties

steps.description: Minimize the cost function J to refine the background by identifying correction functions τ k (z), α k (z), and γ k (z) at each depth level z k.
steps.searchable_summary: minimize cost function to refine background model

steps.description: Deduce a particular age model from a set of correction functions.
steps.searchable_summary: derive age model from correction functions

methods.description: This method investigates the influence of sampling rates and error correlation assumptions on the accuracy of ice core chronology models.

steps.description: Run experiments with markers sampled at uniform rates (100, 200, 300 years) and adaptive sampling (40-140 years).
steps.searchable_summary: conduct experiments with different sampling rates

steps.description: Analyze the influence of error correlation by comparing AddMCE and SqrAddMCE assumptions.
Implement a correlation coefficient that smoothly decreases with the distance between markers.
